main_sections.title: A Friend's Prayer
main_sections.poem_text:
May my friendships always be
The most important thing to me.

With special friends I feel I'm blessed,
So let me give my very best.

I want to do much more than share
The hopes and plans of friends who care;
I'll try all that a friend can do
To make their wishes come true.

Let me use my heart to see,
To realise what friends can be,

And make no judgements from afar,
But love my friends the way they are.

— Jill Wolf
main_sections.poem_appreciation_summary: This short lyric by Jill Wolf is written in the form of a heartfelt prayer addressed to no specific deity but to the idea of friendship itself. The dominant theme is selfless devotion: the speaker wishes not merely to share in friendship but to actively work to make her friends' wishes come true. The tone is warm, earnest, and quietly aspirational. Central imagery includes the heart as an organ of understanding rather than emotion — 'Let me use my heart to see' — suggesting empathy as the true basis of friendship. The poem's most striking idea is unconditional acceptance: 'love my friends the way they are' and 'make no judgements from afar.' The rhyme scheme (AABB) gives the poem the cadence of a simple prayer or vow, reinforcing its devotional quality.

reading_for_comprehension.section_name: Let us read + Let us discuss + Let us think and reflect

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Before reading: Read the following sentences. 1. I wish my friend brings idli in her tiffin tomorrow. 2. I hope my friend and I win the race tomorrow. 3. May my friend get well soon. Which of the three sentences 1–3 is closest to a prayer? Explain to your teacher and classmates.

question_bank.stem: Which of the three sentences is closest to a prayer? Explain.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: What would you pray for your friend? First speak about it and then write it down.
Let us discuss (after reading the poem).

question_bank.stem: Read the poem silently. As you read, underline the parts of the poem that you agree with.
Now, share the details of your underlined parts of the poem in groups and explain why you liked those lines.
Which line was underlined by most people in your group? Share it with your teacher.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Complete the summary of the poem by circling the correct highlighted words. The poet says a prayer for/speech on friendship and friends. She wants that friendship should always be an important part of her complete life/school days. She says that her friendship makes her feel brave/special. She wishes to do the best she can, for her friends. She wants to clap for/listen to the wishes of her friends. As a good friend, she prays/imagines that she is able to make her friends' wishes come true. She wants her heart/mind to understand what a true friend is. She prays that she loves her friends as they are/when they are happy. She tells us that true/correct friends accept their friends for all their qualities.

question_bank.stem: Circle the correct highlighted word in each pair to complete the summary.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Answer in one word only.

question_bank.stem: How does the poet feel about special friends?
What does the poet use to realise what friends can be?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the following lines from the poem and answer the questions. (Stanza 3: 'I want to do much more than share / The hopes and plans of friends who care; / I'll try all that a friend can do / To make their wishes come true.')

question_bank.stem: Complete the sentence with the most suitable option. These lines tell us that the speaker is _______________. A. clever  B. caring  C. curious  D. calm
How will the speaker make her friends' wishes come true? (by trying hard/by working a lot)
Fill in the blank with a suitable word. My friends will feel __________ if their wishes come true.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the following lines from the poem and answer the questions. (Stanza 4–5: 'Let me use my heart to see, / To realise what friends can be, / And make no judgements from afar, / But love my friends the way they are.')

question_bank.stem: The speaker says that feelings are important to understand friendship. Which line tells us this?
Complete the sentence with the most suitable option. These lines tell us that the speaker is _______________. A. helpful  B. thoughtful  C. cheerful  D. hopeful
Fill in the blank with one word. The speaker does not wish to make any ___________. She cares for them just as they are.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Give one reason why we can say that this poem is a prayer.
Why does the speaker 'want to do more' for her friend?
Do you think that the speaker knows about the wishes of her friend? How can we say so?
Do you also pray for your friend? What do you wish for? Discuss.

listening.transcript_text: Hello! Let me tell you what outdoor activities Ajay and Dhiraj enjoy doing. Some days they like playing at the swings in the park. Ajay enjoys slides the most. Dhiraj likes nature, so both take walks in the nearby park, listen to birds and look at the trees. On Saturdays, both go cycling around the neighbourhood. What both truly enjoy is eating ice-cream together. Sometimes they also purchase a comic book, sit on a park bench and read together. They are truly best friends.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You will listen to a talk about some outdoor activities that two friends spend their time doing. As you listen, answer the given question in one or two word(s) only.

question_bank.stem: What do the two friends purchase and use on the park bench?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You will once again listen to the talk about some outdoor activities of the two friends. As you listen, circle the pictures that show these activities. (Six pictures shown: park bench reading, cycling, etc.)

question_bank.stem: Circle the pictures (from the six shown) that match the activities described in the listening passage.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: In pairs, act the role of friends speaking to each other. Speak about the following situations: 1. A walk in the park: Greet each other. Ask if your friend wishes to go with you to the park. If yes, plan some activities. If no, ask what your friend would like to do. Listen and share what you would also like to do together. 2. Sharing tiffins at school: Greet each other. Tell your friends what food items you have brought in your tiffin. Ask them what they have brought. Tell them you wish to share tiffins! Next, share with your friends, how it makes you feel. Also: Tell your friend that you like spending time with each other. Appreciate your friends' food and ask what other food items they like. Thank your friends for sharing.
Create a new situation and have another conversation.

Describe how your friendship with your friend started. First list the answers for — When? Where? How? Then, use your sentences to write a short paragraph of about 80 words. Give a title to your paragraph.
The poet says, 'With special friends I feel I'm blessed'. Select the words that are similar to the meaning of being blessed, from those given below: excited, grateful, understood, thankful, relaxed.

question_bank.stem: Select the words from the given list that are similar in meaning to 'blessed': excited / grateful / understood / thankful / relaxed.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Good friends have a lot of qualities. Let us list some. loyal, h e __ __ f __ l, k __ __ d, c __ __ i n g, h o __ __ __ t. Now, fill in the blanks with the words you just listed above. 1. My friend is always _____________ to me even when I make a mistake. 2. My friend shares things with me. She is very _____________. 3. My friend is _____________ because he always supports me. 4. My friend never lies. She is an _____________ person. 5. My friend and I believe in _____________ for each other.

question_bank.stem: Complete the partial words for qualities of a good friend (loyal, helpful, kind, caring, honest) and then fill in the blanks in the five sentences.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Think of any three of your friends. Write three sentences on why you like each one of them.

There are many quotations about friendship. Read the ones given below: 'A friend in need is a friend indeed.' / 'My best friend is the one who brings out the best in me.' 1. What do you think is the meaning of these quotations? Share it with your teacher. 2. Think of an experience in your life with your friend that suits this quotation. Share it with your friends.

question_bank.stem: What do you think is the meaning of the two friendship quotations? Share with your teacher.
Think of an experience in your life with your friend that suits one of these quotations. Share it.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Look for some famous quotations on friendship; these can be in your own language too. Read and share them with your teacher and classmates. Write one quotation on paper, colour and decorate it. Put it up on the class board.

question_bank.stem: Find a famous friendship quotation (can be in your own language). Write, colour and decorate it on paper for the class board.
